rulings.Case Title: SIMA ZOKAEI VS. SALEM MANSOIR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Petitioners Request For Appellate Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 10, 2025 line 1. RESPONDENT SALEM MANSOIR ALSO KNOWN AS SALIM D. MANSOIR. ALSO KNOWN AS SALIM DONALD MANSOIR, AN INDIVIDUAL Motion To Strike Petitioners Request For Appellate Attorneys Fees is DENIED. Petitioner is entitled to attorney's fees on appeal. Motion to Strike Costs is untimely and is not supported by any argument or authority. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRI CHUNG VS. BAKER PLACES, INC., A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Restec Contractors Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 10, 2025 line 3. CROSS COMPLAINANT FDC CONSTRUCTION, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Restec Contractors Inc. is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. No evidence provided that any claims "arising out of or resulting from performance of the Subcontractor's Work" are brought against the indemnitee. See Separate Statement including 6 facts (and supporting evidence) none of which addresses the "Subcontractor's Work," a capitalized term in the relied-upon indemnification clause. Late-filed opposition is stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BHARAT PATEL VS. JESSIE J. MARQUARD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Vacate Court Dismissal And Request For Order For Trial Continuance Or Re-Setting`
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 10, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF BHARAT PATEL Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Vacate Court Dismissal And Request For Order For Trial Continuance Or Re-Setting` is DENIED. On July 1, 2024 parties were served with Notice of Time and Place of Trial and Settlement Hearing. The trial was set for August 12, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. and the in-person Mandatory Settlement Conference was set for August 7, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. The Court records indicate that Limited Scope Representation was ONLY for August 12, 2024 hearing (with no mention of August 7, 2024 mandatory settlement conference). The Limited Scope Representation reflecting August 12, 2024 appearance bears the signature of Bharat Patel. The Notice of Remote appearance was also made for August 12, 2024 ONLY. The case was dismissed for failure to appear at the Mandatory Settlement Conference on August 7, 2024 that is not mentioned in any of the filings submitted by Limited Scope counsel. While there appears to be some confusion on Plaintiff's part regarding appearance by zoom on August 12, 2024, that appearance did not cause the dismissal. Failure to appear at the mandatory settlement conference on August 7, 2024 caused the dismissal and this failure is not addressed in the motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 10, 2025 line 6. DEFENDANT JOSEPHINE PILLA DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges service of the operative notice in compliance with CCP 1166(a)(5) and is not uncertain. Defendant failed to set the demurrer hearing on proper notice accounting for the minimum notice and service by mail.
